ops.title: Shared AGENTS.md fragments live ONLY in cc-skills guides/ — cc-guides renders fetch them by immutable sha at render time; a content edit needs just a cc-skills push (no cc-guides release, no dual-copy sync)

ops.body:
As of the 2026-07-09 layout-composition cutover (cc-guides 0.1.10+), shared guide content lives in ONE place: `cc-skills/guides/md/*.md`. The old regime — byte-identical copies in `cc-guides/fragments/md/` go:embed'd into the binary, "edit both, cmp to verify, release cc-guides BEFORE pushing cc-skills" — is dead; `cc-guides/fragments/` no longer exists.

- Consumers compose artifacts from `.claude/fragments/<target>/layout.toml` mixing repo-local `*.fragment.md` pieces with imports like `cc-skills:ccx`. An import resolves to an immutable commit via `git ls-remote` at render time, fetched from codeload and cached; the banner stamps the sha (`fragments=cc-skills@<sha>`).
- Propagation for a content edit: push cc-skills `guides/**` → `guides-fan-out.yml` dispatches `cc-guides-render` to every repo in `fleet.json` (21 repos); each render picks up the new sha and commits. No cc-guides binary release is involved unless renderer LOGIC changed.
- Still true: never hand-run a local `cc-guides render` in a consumer to "help" (stale brew binary regresses content); `check` reproduces offline from the banner sha.
- 2026-07-10: ccx fragment updated this way (dependency-source entry 12, anchored-path grep semantics, rg carve-out) — cc-skills 1d12978b. [[ccx-grep-engine-guard-rewrites]] [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]]
- Pull-model phase 1 (cc-skills 1ba9a8b): `.claude/cc-guides.toml` names `plugin/guides` as the pack home, so `guides/` and `plugin/guides/` are byte-identical copies edited in LOCKSTEP — fan-out triggers on `guides/**`, the lint gate on `plugin/guides/**`; a one-copy edit either doesn't propagate or fails lint. Ship-wording update went this way (cc-skills 9d162c48; end-to-end propagation ~2 min push→fleet render commit).
- cc-skills' OWN AGENTS.md is not on the pipeline (not in fleet.json, no fragments layout, stale `_partials` canonical stamp) — hand-patched ship line only; cc-skills backlog b94418d tracks onboarding it.
- jj trap (still applies): in a shared working copy based behind main, `jj commit <fileset>` carves onto the STALE base; fix with `jj rebase -r <change> -d main`, then address by CHANGE id.
